task: Fix 'move to' dropdown menu in business tab

root_causes.description: Most views (BusinessView, InvestmentsView, ActivityView, YouTubeView) passed 'entryId' prop, but EntryDetail expected 'entry' and 'isOpen' props
root_causes.impact: Component would not display correctly when opened from these views

root_causes.description: No UI or API integration existed to move entries between categories
root_causes.impact: Users could not move entries from one category to another (e.g., business → investments)

changes.description: Added dual-mode support for EntryDetail component
changes.details: Component now accepts EITHER (entryId + onClose) OR (entry + isOpen + onClose) props, maintaining backward compatibility with DashboardView while fixing BusinessView and other views
Added dropdown button in footer with options: YouTube, Business, Investments, Activity. Current category shown as disabled. Dropdown closes when clicking outside.
Calls PUT /api/entries/:id with new category. Requires X-API-Key header from localStorage. Shows loading state, success message, and auto-closes modal on success.
Component now shows loading spinner when fetching entry by ID, and displays errors if fetch or move operations fail
Changed import from 'sanitizeHtml' to 'sanitizeHTML' to match the actual export name in utils/sanitize.js

state_management.moveError: Stores any error from move operation
state_management.moveSuccess: Tracks successful move for UI feedback

manual_testing_recommended: Click an entry in Business tab to open modal
Click 'Move to' button and select a different category
Verify entry moves and modal closes with success message
Verify moved entry no longer appears in original category view
Test error handling by simulating network failure

backwards_compatibility.notes: DashboardView continues to work with (entry + isOpen + onClose) props. All other views work with (entryId + onClose) props.
